# Plan Checklist: Tracking Mode with Video Clips

**Purpose**: Validate the implementation plan for completeness, clarity, consistency with the spec, and readiness for task generation
**Created**: 2026-03-29
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on the feature spec and implementation plan.

## Spec-to-Plan Traceability

- [x] CHK001 - Does the plan trace every functional requirement from FR-001 through FR-021 to at least one specific design change or planned test, including FR-012 frame-time display and FR-021 non-blocking error handling? [Completeness, Spec §Requirements, Plan §Spec-to-Plan Traceability] → **Resolved**: the traceability table now enumerates FR-001 through FR-021 individually and explicitly covers FR-012 capture-time overlays plus FR-021 create/write/finalize failure handling.
- [x] CHK002 - Are all five success criteria (SC-001 to SC-005) tied to explicit validation steps rather than design intent alone? [Traceability, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Spec-to-Plan Traceability] → **Resolved**: SC-001 through SC-005 now each map to concrete integration/manual validation steps in plan §Spec-to-Plan Traceability.
- [x] CHK003 - Is the plan-introduced `1-30` `Videoclip FPS` range reconciled with the feature requirements so implementation does not add an undocumented user-visible constraint? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Delivery Notes] → **Resolved**: plan §Technical Context, §Change 1, and §Delivery Notes remove the `1-30` cap and keep `videoclip_fps` aligned with the spec’s “positive whole number” requirement.

## Design Completeness

- [x] CHK004 - Does Change 2 define how clip filename collisions are avoided if two sessions ever share the same `YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` timestamp prefix? [Gap, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 now reserves collision-safe clip paths with `-01`, `-02`, ... suffixes.
- [x] CHK005 - Does Change 2 define cleanup behavior for leftover `.partial.avi` files when writer creation, frame writes, or final rename fails? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 now distinguishes zero-frame cleanup, current-session write failure, readable rename-failure recovery, and deletion of empty/unreadable temp files.
- [x] CHK006 - Does the design specify whether a partial clip is still preserved when only the session-start frame was written before interruption? [Coverage, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 4 `abandon(reason)` now preserves opening-frame-only clips whenever the artifact is reviewable.
- [x] CHK007 - Does Change 3 specify whether video-mode sampling uses every processed frame, repeated snapshots, or best-effort latest frames when the requested `Videoclip FPS` exceeds sustainable throughput? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Change 3] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 3 now defines the exact behavior: drop extra processed snapshots when inference is faster than clip cadence, and repeat the latest processed snapshot when clip cadence exceeds sustainable throughput.
- [x] CHK008 - Does Change 4 define how per-frame top-bar timestamps are sourced so they reflect actual frame capture time rather than delayed annotation/write time? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 3 and §Change 4 now pass `captured_at` through detection snapshots and verification callbacks into annotation.
- [x] CHK009 - Does Change 4 specify what happens if sampled frames change resolution mid-session, given `VideoWriter` requires stable dimensions? [Gap, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 now locks writer output size from the first frame and letterboxes later frames to that size.
- [x] CHK010 - Does the plan explicitly state whether video-mode recording continues while Live View is open, so main-window visibility cannot accidentally suppress clip frames through reused screenshot logic? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-017, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 4 explicitly states that video recording continues while Live View is open and is not gated by `_main_window_visible` or screenshot suppression logic.
- [x] CHK011 - Does Change 4 define whether the clip writer starts before or after the immediate `Cat detected` frame is annotated, so the clip always preserves the beginning of the visit? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 and §Change 4 now state that the opening frame is annotated first and then used to open/write frame 1 before the sampler starts.
- [x] CHK012 - Does Change 5 specify a bounded shutdown/finalize sequence so clip cleanup cannot hang app exit beyond the SC-001 10-second target? [Non-Functional, Spec §SC-001, Plan §Change 5] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 5 now defines a bounded cleanup budget with sampler stop and finalize/rename deadlines.

## Design Clarity

- [x] CHK013 - Is the relationship between `detection_fps` and `videoclip_fps` precise enough to be unambiguous when the saved detection fps is already higher than the clip fps? [Clarity, Plan §Change 3] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 3 now explicitly defines both directions: higher `detection_fps` drops unnecessary processed snapshots, higher `videoclip_fps` repeats the latest processed snapshot.
- [x] CHK014 - Does the plan explicitly define whether `videoclip_fps` changes made during an active session are ignored even if the sampler thread is already running? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 1 and §Change 4 now state that active sessions keep the snapshotted mode/fps until the session closes.
- [x] CHK015 - Is the ownership boundary between `annotation.py` and `tracking_video.py` specific enough that annotation, file naming, and finalize error handling are not duplicated across modules? [Clarity, Plan §Project Structure, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Project Structure and §Change 4 now define explicit ownership boundaries between `annotation.py`, `tracking_video.py`, and `main.py`.
- [x] CHK016 - Does the plan define whether `TrackingClipWriter` failures disable recording only for the current session or for all subsequent video sessions until restart? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 and §Change 4 now scope clip-writer failure disablement to the current session only.
- [x] CHK017 - Is the plan explicit about which artifact, if any, is user-visible while a video-mode session is still in progress: temp file, final file only, or no reviewable artifact until finalize? [Gap, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 now states that only the `.partial.avi` may be visible during recording and the final `.avi` appears only after successful finalize.
- [x] CHK018 - Does the plan clarify whether repeated sampled frames carrying the same remained outcome are intentional background context rather than duplicate outcome events? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 3, §Change 4, and §Delivery Notes now define repeated frames as continuity, not extra outcome transitions.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK019 - Are sessions crossing midnight handled consistently in both storage layout and in-frame local times from session start through clip finalization? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 anchors storage paths to the session-start timestamp, while §Change 3 and §Change 4 render each frame from its own capture timestamp.
- [x] CHK020 - Does the plan address what happens if the tracking directory is writable at session start but becomes unavailable during later frame writes or the final rename? [Coverage, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Change 5] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 now defines write/rename failure handling and readable-temp preservation, and §Change 5 keeps monitoring alive while surfacing a non-blocking error.
- [x] CHK021 - Does the plan define behavior for a session that ends before the sampler thread produces its first periodic tick, beyond the forced start and verification frames? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-016, Plan §Change 4] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 4 explicitly states that forced start plus forced verification frames are sufficient when no periodic sampler tick occurs.
- [x] CHK022 - Is it defined what makes a partial clip “reviewable” for SC-005 if finalization succeeds but the container may still fail to open on some platforms? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-005, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 2 and §Delivery Notes define “reviewable” as OpenCV being able to read at least one frame, with packaged-player compatibility left as an explicit manual boundary.

## Test Plan Completeness

- [x] CHK023 - Does the test plan include a case proving the per-frame top-bar time comes from frame capture time, not delayed render/finalize time? [Gap, Spec §FR-012, Plan §Test Plan] → **Resolved**: plan §Test Plan adds both unit and integration coverage for capture-time overlays.
- [x] CHK024 - Does the test plan include same-second session filename collision coverage or an explicit rationale for why it is impossible? [Edge Case, Plan §Test Plan, Plan §Change 2] → **Resolved**: plan §Test Plan now includes explicit same-second collision tests for `reserve_tracking_clip_paths()` and integration-level `-01` clip creation.
- [x] CHK025 - Does the test plan include a failed-finalize or failed-rename scenario for `.partial.avi` cleanup and user-visible error reporting? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Test Plan] → **Resolved**: plan §Test Plan now covers finalize-rename failure, readable `.partial.avi` preservation, and non-blocking error surfacing.
- [x] CHK026 - Does the test plan include a mid-session settings save proving the active clip keeps its original mode/fps while the next session uses the new values? [Coverage, Spec §FR-019, Plan §Test Plan] → **Resolved**: plan §Test Plan adds unit and integration checks for mid-session save behavior and next-session adoption.
- [x] CHK027 - Does the test plan include a low-throughput scenario where configured `Videoclip FPS` exceeds sustainable processing, so degradation behavior is documented and measurable? [Gap, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Test Plan] → **Resolved**: plan §Test Plan now includes a low-throughput integration case with repeated continuity frames.
- [x] CHK028 - Does the test plan include normal tray-exit shutdown, not just direct `abandon()`, to confirm partial clips survive the real app-exit path introduced in Change 5? [Coverage, Spec §FR-020, Spec §SC-005, Plan §Change 5, Plan §Test Plan] → **Resolved**: plan §Project Structure and §Test Plan add `tests/integration/test_main_shutdown.py` for tray-exit and related real cleanup paths.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 - Is the assumption that OpenCV `MJPG` + `.avi` is sufficiently cross-platform for packaged CatGuard builds documented with measurable acceptance criteria or an explicit manual verification boundary? [Assumption, Plan §Change 2, Plan §Manual verification] → **Resolved**: plan §Technical Context, §Change 2, §Manual verification, and §Delivery Notes now state that OpenCV readback is the automated bar and packaged-player compatibility is a manual release check.
- [x] CHK030 - Does the plan make clear whether `get_latest_detection_snapshot()` needs new synchronization guarantees beyond the existing frame lock, since boxes and frames must stay paired for FR-017? [Dependency, Spec §FR-017, Plan §Change 3] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 3 now makes the existing `_frame_lock` the atomic synchronization point for frame, boxes, timestamp, and sequence.
- [x] CHK031 - Is the dependency on “one active session at a time” validated against all entry points that can start or end tracking, including manual pause, schedule stop, camera error, shutdown, and resumed detection? [Consistency, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Change 4, Plan §Change 5] → **Resolved**: plan §Change 5 now enumerates manual pause, schedule stop, camera error, tray exit, signal shutdown, and resume behavior, and explicitly restarts sessions fresh after resume.

## Notes

- All 31 items resolved as of 2026-03-31.
- The main changes were removal of the undocumented `1-30` cap, explicit capture-time/timer semantics, collision-safe clip paths, readable-partial recovery rules, and real shutdown-path coverage.
- Plan is ready for `/speckit.tasks`.
